name: couchbase
explanation: Couchbase exposes a REST API on port 8091 and the auto_conf template already covers host/port via `http://%%host%%:8091`. However, every Couchbase deployment beyond a fresh install requires admin credentials (username/password) to access `/pools`, `/pools/default`, bucket stats, query stats, and index stats. The HTTP_CONFIG_REMAPPER in the check explicitly remaps `user` to `username`, confirming auth is the standard mode. Credentials cannot be discovered from the wire, so classification is impossible.
notes: Spec marks username/password optional because they live in the shared HTTP template, but in practice the Couchbase REST API rejects unauthenticated stats requests.
